prompt: Parse a binary WAV audio file located at /root/audio_sample.wav. Split the audio into 1-second segments and compute the RMS (Root Mean Square) amplitude of each segment. Identify the segment with the highest RMS amplitude. Save the analysis to /root/amplitude_report.json with keys: sample_rate (integer), num_channels (integer), total_duration_sec (float), total_segments (integer), segments (list of objects with segment_index, start_sec, end_sec, rms_amplitude), loudest_segment_index (integer, 0-based index of the loudest segment). The result will be considered correct if RMS values are within 0.01% relative tolerance.
constraints: Parse WAV binary format directly without relying on external audio libraries beyond the standard library
The verifier should accept results within 0.01% relative tolerance
